skill.name: docx
skill.description: Create, inspect, edit, and validate Microsoft Word .docx files using OOXML, docx-js, tracked changes, comments, and document conversion workflows.
skill.summary: Work with professional Word documents while preserving structure, comments, and tracked changes.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many issues, and most schema URL, weak-crypto, C2 keyword, and Markdown backtick hits appear to be false positives from OOXML schemas or documentation examples. However, executable helpers perform unchecked ZIP extraction on user-controlled Office files and invoke local document tools, so the skill should not be published until archive extraction is hardened.

high_findings.title: Unchecked Office Archive Extraction
high_findings.description: Office documents are ZIP archives, and helper scripts call extractall on user-provided files without validating member paths. A malicious archive could write files outside the intended output directory through Zip Slip path traversal.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged files contain direct archive extraction of Office files, which are expected to come from users. No evidence of path normalization or member validation was found at these extraction locations.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution for Document Tooling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run tools such as pandoc, Python scripts, LibreOffice, Poppler, npm, and apt-get. Some Python helpers also invoke subprocess.run with fixed command arrays for validation and diffing; this is expected for document workflows but increases operational risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and subprocess calls are real and semantically tied to document processing. Arguments are mostly fixed arrays rather than shell strings, so command injection evidence is limited.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Modification Scope
medium_findings.description: The document library copies, writes, packs, and deletes files while creating or modifying Office documents. This is legitimate functionality, but it requires users to run the skill only in trusted workspaces and with controlled input paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain expected filesystem operations for Office document creation and editing. I found no evidence of deliberate exfiltration, but the operations affect user-controlled workspaces.

low_findings.title: Static Schema and Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Many high and critical static hits are caused by OOXML schema vocabulary, namespace URLs, long XML template lines, Markdown code fences, and references to legacy hash names inside schemas or document metadata. These do not show malicious intent by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged locations are schemas, XML templates, or Markdown examples, not hidden executable payloads. The executable risk is better represented by the separate archive and subprocess findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: zipfile.extractall Without Path Validation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper scripts extract complete Office archives without checking each archive member stays inside the destination directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present at the listed lines and applies to user-supplied Office archives. This is a well-known archive traversal risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Calls to Local Document Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The validation and redlining helpers invoke external binaries such as soffice and git. The subprocess calls use argument arrays, but they still execute local programs on generated or user-derived files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess usage is real, but the observed calls use fixed executable names and list arguments. The concern is sandbox and file handling exposure, not confirmed command injection.

content.user_title: Edit Word Documents with Track Changes
content.value_statement: Professional Word documents are hard to edit safely because formatting, comments, and tracked changes live inside OOXML files. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows for creating, reviewing, editing, validating, and repacking .docx files.
actual_capabilities: Create new .docx files with docx-js and structured Word elements.
Unpack .docx files and inspect raw OOXML document parts.
Apply tracked insertions, deletions, comments, and replies through helper libraries.
Convert Word documents to Markdown for text review and change verification.
Repack edited Office document folders into .docx, .pptx, or .xlsx archives.
Validate edited documents with schema checks and local conversion tools.
limitations: Requires local tools such as Python, pandoc, LibreOffice, Poppler, and npm packages.
Works directly on Office archive contents, so untrusted files need sandboxing.
Complex Word formatting can require manual OOXML inspection and targeted fixes.
The bundled unpack helpers need safer ZIP path validation before publication.

use_cases.title: Contract Redline Review
use_cases.description: Apply precise tracked changes and comments to legal or business documents while preserving review history.

use_cases.title: Document Generation
use_cases.description: Create formatted Word files from structured content, section outlines, and reusable document components.

use_cases.title: OOXML Troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Inspect document XML, repair structure problems, and validate files after automated edits.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Document Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a Word file
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this .docx file to Markdown with tracked changes visible, then summarize the main sections and open review items.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Word Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a .docx proposal from this outline. Use headings, bullet lists, a title page, and consistent paragraph styling.

prompt_templates.title: Add Review Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Open this .docx file, add comments to the unclear clauses, and preserve the original document text and formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Tracked Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply these negotiated edits as tracked changes in the .docx file. Batch related changes, verify each batch, and produce a final validation summary.

output_examples.input: A contract draft with requested edits to payment terms and termination language.
output_examples.output: A reviewed Word document with tracked insertions, deletions, comments, and a short verification summary.

output_examples.input: A product brief outline with section headings and bullet points.
output_examples.output: A formatted .docx draft with a title, headings, paragraphs, lists, and consistent document structure.

output_examples.input: A corrupted or heavily edited .docx file that needs inspection.
output_examples.output: An OOXML review report that identifies document parts, validation errors, and recommended repair steps.

best_practices: Process untrusted Office files in a sandboxed workspace with limited permissions.
Use tracked changes for edits to third-party, legal, academic, business, or government documents.
Verify final documents by converting them to Markdown and checking that intended changes appear.
anti_patterns: Do not extract arbitrary .docx files into sensitive directories.
Do not replace full paragraphs when only a small phrase should be redlined.
Do not skip validation after direct OOXML edits.

faq.question: Can this skill create new Word documents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a docx-js workflow for creating structured .docx files from JavaScript or TypeScript.

faq.question: Can it preserve tracked changes?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflows include tracked insertion, deletion, comment, and redlining patterns.

faq.question: Does it require Microsoft Word?
faq.answer: No. It uses OOXML, Python, pandoc, LibreOffice, and related command-line tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to use on untrusted documents?
faq.answer: Use caution. The current archive extraction helpers need path validation, so sandbox untrusted files.

faq.question: Can it edit comments and replies?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundled document library includes methods for adding comments and replies.

faq.question: Can it validate edited documents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes schema validation and conversion-based checks for edited Office files.
